component_details.name: CometBFT
component_details.description: General-purpose blockchain consensus engine used by BeaconKit.

asset_details.name: Liquidity
asset_details.description: Tokens available in liquidity pools for trading.

behavior_details.name: Swap Assets
behavior_details.description: External User exchanges one token for another on BEX.

data_flow_details.description: BEX sends swapped tokens or LP tokens back to the user wallet.

boundary_details.name: Berachain Bridge to Berachain
boundary_details.description: Separates the external bridging service from the core blockchain network.

security_gap.gap_description: Vote transaction does not strongly verify the sender's identity against their authorized voting power.

actor_risk.description: An external user can send transactions to the governance contract.

actor_details.name: External User
actor_details.description: General user interacting with the Berachain ecosystem.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Successful spoofing of votes could manipulate governance outcomes.

assumptions: The governance contract's vote processing does not adequately check transaction sender against authorized voting power.
An attacker can forge or replay signed vote transactions.

mitigation.strategy: Implement robust signature verification and nonce checks for vote transactions.

description: An attacker sends vote transactions impersonating other users to sway governance proposal outcomes.

origin.capability_match: Add Incentives To A Reward Vault

component_details.name: User Wallet
component_details.description: Software or hardware storing user private keys and interacting with the blockchain.

asset_details.name: Private Key
asset_details.description: Cryptographic key used to access and control assets in a wallet.

asset_details.name: Voting Power
asset_details.description: Influence in governance decisions, derived from BGT holdings/delegation.

behavior_details.name: Burn HONEY
behavior_details.description: External User burns $HONEY to redeem collateral assets on Honey Swap.

data_flow_details.description: User stakes PoL-eligible tokens in a Reward Vault.

boundary_details.name: Berachain Bridge to Berachain
boundary_details.description: Separates the external bridging service from the core blockchain network.

security_gap.gap_description: Incentive token manager can add incentives with rates below the whitelisted minimum or use non-whitelisted tokens.

actor_risk.description: The external user is acting in the role of an Incentive Token Manager for a Reward Vault.

actor_details.name: External User
actor_details.description: General user interacting with the Berachain ecosystem.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Manipulated incentives can disincentivize validators and disrupt the PoL mechanism for users.

assumptions: The Reward Vault contract or associated logic does not enforce the minimum whitelisted incentive rate.
The Reward Vault contract allows deposits of non-whitelisted incentive tokens.

mitigation.strategy: Validate incentive rate against governance minimum and confirm token whitelisting in the add incentive function.

description: An Incentive Token Manager adds incentives at a malicious rate or with unauthorized tokens, undermining the incentive marketplace.

trace_source.matched_capability: Add Incentives To A Reward Vault

component_details.name: Berachain Bridge
component_details.description: Canonical bridge powered by LayerZero for transferring assets between chains.

asset_details.name: Private Key
asset_details.description: Cryptographic key used to access and control assets in a wallet.

behavior_details.name: Trade Tokens
behavior_details.description: External User trades tokens on a Centralized Exchange.

data_flow_details.description: User withdraws tokens from a centralized exchange to their wallet.

boundary_details.name: Berachain Bridge to Berachain
boundary_details.description: Separates the external bridging service from the core blockchain network.

security_gap.gap_description: Lack of clear on-chain record linking the claim transaction to the user's identity and proof.

actor_risk.description: Standard external user performing a claim action.

actor_details.name: External User
actor_details.description: General user interacting with the Berachain ecosystem.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Standard blockchain transactions inherently provide non-repudiation via signatures and public logs.

assumptions: The claim transaction signature is not properly recorded or verifiable against the user's identity.
The proof used in the claim cannot be linked back to the specific user on-chain.

mitigation.strategy: Ensure all claim transactions are signed and permanently recorded on the blockchain, linking the signature to the user's address and the specific claim proof used.

description: A user claims incentives and later denies having performed the claim transaction.

component_details.name: CometBFT
component_details.description: General-purpose blockchain consensus engine used by BeaconKit.

asset_details.name: Liquidity
asset_details.description: Tokens available in liquidity pools for trading.

behavior_details.name: Swap Assets
behavior_details.description: External User exchanges one token for another on BEX.

data_flow_details.description: BEX sends swapped tokens or LP tokens back to the user wallet.

boundary_details.name: Berachain Bridge to Berachain
boundary_details.description: Separates the external bridging service from the core blockchain network.

security_gap.gap_description: Governance contract or network can be overloaded by excessive transaction volume.

actor_risk.description: An external user can submit a high volume of transactions.

actor_details.name: External User
actor_details.description: General user interacting with the Berachain ecosystem.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Spamming can prevent legitimate users from participating in governance (voting) or increase transaction costs.

assumptions: Transaction fees are low enough, or transaction processing limits are high enough, to make spamming feasible and impactful.
Contract logic is not optimized to efficiently handle a high volume of certain calls.

mitigation.strategy: Tune gas costs, implement network-level transaction rate limits, and optimize contract function execution cost.

description: An attacker floods the network or the governance contract with excessive vote transactions, disrupting the voting process.

origin.capability_match: Add Incentives To A Reward Vault

asset_details.name: Private Key
asset_details.description: Cryptographic key used to access and control assets in a wallet.

asset_details.description: Influence in governance decisions, derived from BGT holdings/delegation.

behavior_details.description: External User burns $HONEY to redeem collateral assets on Honey Swap.

data_flow_details.description: User stakes PoL-eligible tokens in a Reward Vault.

boundary_details.name: Berachain Bridge to Berachain
boundary_details.description: Separates the external bridging service from the core blockchain network.

security_gap.gap_description: Compromise of the Incentive Token Manager's private key grants an attacker unauthorized control over adding incentives.

actor_risk.description: The external user holds the privileged role of Incentive Token Manager.

actor_details.name: External User
actor_details.description: General user interacting with the Berachain ecosystem.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Unauthorized use of the Token Manager role can lead to malicious or disruptive incentive distribution.

assumptions: The private key for the Incentive Token Manager account is compromised.
The Incentive Token Manager role has significant power over incentive distribution.

mitigation.strategy: Require multi-signature authorization for the Token Manager role or use hardware security modules for key storage.

description: An attacker gains control of the Incentive Token Manager's account and uses their privileges to add malicious incentives.

trace_source.matched_capability: Add Incentives To A Reward Vault
